decisions.text: Scope is at the program level; not at tenant-level.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §2 Key Product Decisions #1

decisions.text: For Clinical Course, preceptor and site evaluations stay within learning activities — CE for clinical courses focuses only on course content/structure, the course coordinator (DCE), and Course Instructor.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §2 Key Product Decisions #3

decisions.text: Survey responses are anonymous: no student identifier is attached to the submitted response.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §4.3 Step 3 — Response Collection

decisions.text: Minimum response threshold is 5 — results for any course suppressed until minimum 5 responses received.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §9 Non-Functional Requirements

decisions.text: 1–5 Likert scale enforced as standard across all evaluations. Not a configurable option at faculty or program level.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §9 Non-Functional Requirements

decisions.text: Evaluation data retained for minimum 7 years to support longitudinal accreditation evidence.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §9 Non-Functional Requirements

decisions.text: The overall approach is to re-design Surveys in such a way that Course Evaluation and Survey can co-exist.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §2 Key Product Decisions #4

decisions.text: Three new constructs introduced to enable CE: Survey type (mandatory — Course evaluations or General surveys), Course type (optional — Didactic or Clinical), and Subject (assigned to each section).
decisions.source: PCE PRD §2 Key Product Decisions #5

decisions.text: We will just track submission and not the responses — survey responses are untraceable to individual students; no IP address, device ID, or response timing metadata exposed.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §9 Non-Functional Requirements — Anonymity/Privacy

decisions.text: FERPA does not apply to student-to-faculty course feedback — student feedback is voluntary and not classified as academic data (grades, scores, recordings). Faculty will not know the names of the students who submitted feedback. Anonymity is a design principle, not a FERPA requirement in this context.
decisions.source: Course evaluation and survey design — base entities, template workflow, and ETAs transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Template 'subjects' are populated from the program's Prism course-level role associations — the same lookup used by course role configuration. No separate subject list is maintained in PCE. If the program adds a new role, it flows through Prism-based configuration and auto-appears in PCE subject selection.
decisions.source: Post-course evaluation template — workflow, role-based access, and distribution setup with Monel transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: A template section for a given subject will not render to the student if that subject (role) does not exist in the course offering. The same template can be used across all courses — sections appear or disappear based on the course's role configuration. This eliminates the need for separate templates per course type.
decisions.source: Post-course evaluation template — workflow, role-based access, and distribution setup with Monel transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: PCE distribution must always be tied to a course — no distribution without course association. This is the key differentiator from general surveys. Faculty who want to push a survey without a course association must use the general survey tool.
decisions.source: Post-course evaluation template — workflow, role-based access, and distribution setup with Monel transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Phase 1 will not include predefined out-of-the-box templates. Users start with an empty template. Three creation paths: import an existing evaluation document (PDF/Word) via AI parsing, build manually question by question, or (future) pick from question bank. No vendor-provided starter templates.
decisions.source: Post-course evaluation template — workflow, role-based access, and distribution setup with Monel transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Distribution filters for course selection: term and academic year are required; cohort is optional and used by lockstep programs. These three filters together identify the course offerings to distribute to. For standalone (non-Prism) users, a quick-entry path for course name, number, term, and academic year must be provided.
decisions.source: Post-course evaluation template — workflow, role-based access, and distribution setup with Monel transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: If a template has a course type (Didactic or Clinical) assigned, it auto-assigns to matching course offerings during distribution. If no course type is set in the template, manual course-type assignment is required per course during distribution setup.
decisions.source: Post-course evaluation template — workflow, role-based access, and distribution setup with Monel transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Guest lecturer evaluation is out of scope for PCE phase 1. It remains student-initiated and is handled via the general survey tool. Course coordinators initiating guest lecturer feedback can use PCE in a future phase once reliable event-based distribution is supported.
decisions.source: Post-course evaluation template — workflow, role-based access, and distribution setup with Monel transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Exam download capability is in scope for phase 1 — students must be able to download the exam to their local device within a coordinator-defined download window before the exam. Taking the exam must not require active Internet once downloaded. An explicit, actionable download prompt must be shown to students (not automatic background download).
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026; confirmed in Assessment PRD — accessibility standards, offline exam download, and parity with ExamSoft transcript — May 21, 2026

decisions.text: The question bank exists at the base course level, not the course offering level. All offerings of the same base course share one question bank. Questions added during assessment creation or directly in the bank always save at the base course level — never at the offering level.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Question-level review and approval workflow is out of scope for at least 1 year minimum. Assessment-level review is in scope for phase 1. The review status checkbox currently shown in the question bank table must be removed.
decisions.source: Question bank design — move/copy options, design system alignment, and setup entities transcript — May 20, 2026

decisions.text: Phase 1 UI does not allow creating assessments outside a course context. All assessments must be created within a course. The backend must be architected to accommodate standalone assessments in the future, but no frontend capability will be built in phase 1.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Three roles for exam management: Admin (institution-wide superuser), Course Coordinator (superuser for their course — creates assessments, manages sections, views results), and Collaborator (flexible contributor — can add/edit questions in a course they are assigned to). The Collaborator role does not grant access to student results.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Review function is entirely separate from results access. A reviewer can give feedback on assessment questions before publishing but cannot view student scores or performance data. Only Admin and Course Coordinator (Director) roles can view student results. A reviewer's status per assessment is tracked independently.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Assignment assessment type is deprioritized for phase 1. Faculty use LMS (e.g., Canvas) for assignments and import scores there. Exam and Quiz are the primary supported assessment types. Assignment can be added to the backend, but no UI capability in phase 1.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: AI tagging runs in the background for all questions. When manual tagging exists, it takes precedence over AI-generated tags. Both are visible — AI tags shown with an AI indicator, manual tags shown prominently. The system always maintains AI tags but defers to human judgment when there is a conflict.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Question rationale (explanation of the correct answer) is stored at the question bank level — never at the assessment level. It is editable within the assessment creation workflow, but all edits save back to the source question in the question bank. If rationale is missing, the assessment review screen must flag it.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Option position randomization within a question (locking individual option positions) is an ExamSoft parity feature and must be included in assessment setup configuration for phase 1. This is separate from question-level randomization (already captured) and section-level randomization.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026

decisions.text: Assessment creation supports exactly 4 methods: (1) Copy existing assessment — the most common use case (faculty recycle 5–15% of questions from previous exams); (2) AI prompt-based generation — faculty describe the assessment in natural language and AI selects questions from the bank; (3) Import PDF/document — AI parses an existing exam paper and creates the assessment; (4) Manual selection from question bank. No matter which method is used, the final review and publish steps are identical.
decisions.source: Assessment creation workflows and question bank design transcript — May 19, 2026; confirmed in Assessment question design — AI features, scoring, and workflow transcript — May 22, 2026

decisions.text: Phase 1 accessibility targets for the exam-taking experience: 200% magnification, text-to-speech (pending ExamSoft parity check), dyslexic font support, and high contrast color ratios. Accessibility compliance is acceptable to defer for the January launch but must reach parity with ExamSoft by phase 2 at latest.
decisions.source: Assessment PRD — accessibility standards, offline exam download, and parity with ExamSoft transcript — May 21, 2026

decisions.text: Point-by-serial score in the question bank table displays as plain text, not a bar chart. Aarti and Vishaka reviewed and rejected the bar concept.
decisions.source: Question bank design — move/copy options, design system alignment, and setup entities transcript — May 20, 2026

decisions.text: FAAS UI will be used as-is for question creation in the PCE template builder. The trade-off is user experience — FAAS UI follows the old design system while CE is built on the new design system.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §12 Dependencies and Risks — FAAS UI

decisions.text: Three entry points for CE results viewing: (1) by term — shows all courses in the term with per-term and per-course stats; (2) by faculty — shows all courses a faculty member has taught with per-faculty aggregated stats; (3) by course — shows all historical offerings of a course with per-course aggregated stats. The final 'evaluation card' (single course-offering result view) is identical regardless of which entry point is used.
decisions.source: Course and faculty evaluation setup — directories, templates, and term workflow transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: Directory pages (student, faculty, course master list, course offering) within the CE module are VIEW-ONLY. They pull data from Prism base. No student/faculty/course management workflows are built inside CE. When a user needs to perform a data-management action (e.g., register a student to a course, add faculty to a course), they are deep-linked to the appropriate Prism page in a new tab. The underlying data resides only in Prism — CE never duplicates it.
decisions.source: Course and faculty evaluation setup — directories, templates, and term workflow transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: Students do not get a login, profile creation, or invitation for course evaluations. Distribution is email-only: student email addresses are used to send an initial notification and reminders with a direct link. Students click the link to access and fill the evaluation. No student-facing authentication or account setup is required for CE participation.
decisions.source: Course and faculty evaluation setup — directories, templates, and term workflow transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: Two reusable email templates exist in the CE setup: (1) initial notification sent when a survey opens, and (2) a reminder email template (dynamic — same template reused for all reminder cycles). The default reminder schedule is anchored on term end date (e.g., 15 days before term end). No separate template per reminder cycle is needed.
decisions.source: Course and faculty evaluation setup — directories, templates, and term workflow transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: Academic year and term configuration — with start and end dates for each term — is a mandatory prerequisite for CE distribution automation. Without dates, the system cannot calculate when to open evaluations, send reminders, or close surveys. The CE setup section must require this configuration before a term can be enabled for CE. Terms with missing dates are excluded from the term-enablement workflow.
decisions.source: Course and faculty evaluation setup — directories, templates, and term workflow transcript — Jun 10, 2026; confirmed in India discussion — Weekly Product Sync | New Initiatives (Prism) transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: The CE dashboard groups evaluations by term (not 'project'). Term is the primary organizing concept throughout the CE module — the landing page, dashboard, distribution flow, and analytics all anchor on the current term. Each term group shows its associated course offerings with KPIs and completion stats.
decisions.source: India discussion — Weekly Product Sync | New Initiatives (Prism) transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: Longitudinal analytics requires two dedicated sidebar entry points: (1) Faculty — a faculty directory with leaderboard showing top/bottom performing faculty, search, and a detail view with spider graph (per-section scores), trend chart (historical rating), peer comparison, and per-course breakdown; (2) Course — a course directory showing all historical offerings of each course, with aggregated performance stats and click-through to individual offering results. These two pages are the key differentiators of PCE from a general survey tool.
decisions.source: India discussion — Weekly Product Sync | New Initiatives (Prism) transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: Template sections have three categories: (1) Course content — questions about the course itself; (2) Faculty/roles — questions for faculty, with a multi-select of which program-level faculty roles from Prism this section applies to (all selected roles share the same questions in that section); (3) General — a catch-all section for questions not tied to a specific person or the course content. Multiple sections of each category can be added. The subject/role lookup is powered by program-level faculty role associations in Prism.
decisions.source: India discussion — Weekly Product Sync | New Initiatives (Prism) transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: Faculty evaluation results must not be visible to other faculty. Faculty members should not be able to see each other's scores. This is an explicit design requirement for the CE module, addressing a known limitation of the current Prism product where all faculty can view each other's course evaluation data.
decisions.source: India discussion — Weekly Product Sync | New Initiatives (Prism) transcript — Jun 10, 2026

decisions.text: Template creation: 'Build new' has two sub-options — (a) manually add sections or (b) import from a PDF or Word document (AI parses and creates a draft template). The document import option is presented at the top-level template creation screen, not buried per section. No predefined vendor-provided starter templates are included in phase 1. The 'pick from question bank' path remains a future capability. Note: whether 'Copy existing template' is a supported path is unresolved — see DECISION CONFLICT in docs/watch/flags/pce.md dated 2026-06-09.
decisions.source: Post-Course Survey Cadence Meeting (BiWeely 45 Mins) transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: When adding sections to a template (Build new path): all desired sections/roles can be multi-selected in one action — no need to add one section at a time. Section descriptions are removed; only role/subject labels are shown. There is no per-section PDF import option (document import is top-level only). After choosing 'Build new', the user goes directly into the section-editing interface without additional intermediate option screens.
decisions.source: Post-Course Survey Cadence Meeting (BiWeely 45 Mins) transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: PCE distribution channel is Exact Prism only — no anonymous public link for course evaluations. The anonymous link option remains in the general survey tool only. Each student is permitted to submit only once per course evaluation, enforced by the backend using the Prism student roster for that course offering.
decisions.source: Post-Course Survey Cadence Meeting (BiWeely 45 Mins) transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: Anonymous response is a non-configurable default in CE — not an option the admin chooses. The distribution UI communicates 'responses are anonymous' as a statement to the admin, not as a toggle or question. The visibility/privacy toggle (private vs. share with program) shown in the general survey tool is removed from the CE distribution flow.
decisions.source: Post-Course Survey Cadence Meeting (BiWeely 45 Mins) transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: Comment moderation is a stretch goal for phase 1. If comment moderation is excluded: the results release date field becomes mandatory (not optional) in the distribution setup — the system uses this date to automatically distribute results to stakeholders. If no date is provided, the admin must manually share results; the UI must communicate this explicitly. Results must not be released to faculty before they have submitted final grades — the system cannot enforce this but must include a messaging reminder (soft, not a hard block).
decisions.source: Post-Course Survey Cadence Meeting (BiWeely 45 Mins) transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: Bulk distribution supports two levels of date override: (1) parent (term group) level — date changes cascade to all course instances in the same term distribution; (2) individual course instance level — override for a specific course without affecting others. Both granularities must be supported. The parent-level override is the primary workflow for extending survey windows across an entire term.
decisions.source: Post-Course Survey Cadence Meeting (BiWeely 45 Mins) transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: The Report Access step is removed from the CE distribution wizard in phase 1. Role-based access (program director and above) controls who can view all survey results — no per-survey access configuration is needed. If an admin wants to share specific evaluation data (e.g., a TA's results with a coordinator), they can manually download and email it. Phase 1 must remain simple: no custom report access rules per survey distribution. This resolves the PRD §4 vs. §13c.2 conflict flagged on 2026-06-09.
decisions.source: Post-Course Survey Cadence Meeting (BiWeely 45 Mins) transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: The general survey (programmatic survey) module UI, workflow, and backend are unchanged in this phase. Only the navigation entry point changes — CE and General Surveys are accessible as separate sidebar tabs within the same top-level module. No functional changes to the existing general survey workflow without a strong rationale and explicit approval, as users have already learned the current system and changing it mid-flight creates retraining burden.
decisions.source: Post-Course Survey Cadence Meeting (BiWeely 45 Mins) transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: The course offering landing page in phase 1 is limited to information that currently exists in the system — no future-state data. Performance analytics, accommodations, and interventions are explicitly excluded from the initial build and will be introduced incrementally as those features are built in later phases. The design must not anticipate data that does not yet exist.
decisions.source: Exam Management Sync up transcript — Jun 9, 2026

decisions.text: Focus on selling this module independently in Q2, 2027.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §2 Key Product Decisions #6

decisions.text: No Student login for the CE module until Q3, 2027. Students access CE surveys via email link only (no portal login) through at least Q2 2027.
decisions.source: PCE PRD §2 Key Product Decisions #7

decisions.text: Confirmed Exam Management milestone targets: P0 Cohere delivery 15-Sep 2026, P1 Beta launch 19-Dec 2026, P2 GA Launch 20-Sep (year TBD — estimated 2027 given current timeline). These are the first explicit date-bound milestones recorded in Nipun's roadmap.
decisions.source: Exam Mgmt Roadmap — Nipun (Excel manifest) §Planning — synced 2026-06-16
